items.headline: OpenAI/Microsoft AGI clause formally killed Apr 27
items.diff_narrative: **New: OpenAI/Microsoft AGI clause formally killed Apr 27 — non-exclusive license through 2032, revenue share ends 2030, OpenAI cleared on AWS** — Previous report covered Mag 7 capex framing and Apr 27 futures slip. Now a structural shift: the **"AGI clause" that has shadowed the partnership for years is gone**; revenue-share payments cap on a calendar boundary (2030) regardless of AGI declaration; Microsoft's ~$135B / 27% stake unchanged but exclusivity vacated. **Direct enabling event for the AWS $38B/$50B-headroom contract and the multi-cloud distribution thesis.**

items.headline: AWS × Anthropic moves down to silicon (Trainium/Graviton) Apr 27 + Claude Cowork in Bedrock + AWS × Meta Graviton-at-scale deal
items.diff_narrative: **New: AWS × Anthropic moves down to silicon (Trainium/Graviton) Apr 27 + Claude Cowork in Bedrock + AWS × Meta Graviton-at-scale deal** — Previous report flagged AgentCore Managed Harness (4-region preview) + CLI (14-region GA) + Skills on Kiro Power. Now the partnership extends to **Anthropic training most-advanced models on AWS Trainium + Graviton co-engineered with Annapurna Labs**; **Claude Cowork lands inside Bedrock** with AWS-resident data; "Claude Platform on AWS" unified surface coming. **Meta separately deploying tens of millions of Graviton cores** for agentic-AI CPU workloads. The Apr 27 weekly roundup is the single most-content-rich AWS post of April.

items.headline: Mag 7 super-week + FOMC Apr 28-29 collapse into the same 48h tape
items.diff_narrative: **New: Mag 7 super-week + FOMC Apr 28-29 collapse into the same 48h tape — Powell's last meeting, rates held 3.50-3.75% (100%), Apple Apr 30, ~$700B 2026 capex** — Previous report covered Mag 7 earnings week + Hormuz futures slip + Fed unchanged. Now the **calendar coupling tightens**: FOMC is Apr 28-29; Microsoft/Alphabet/Amazon/Meta print **after the close Apr 29** (same day as Fed); Apple Apr 30. **Powell term ends May 15** — Warsh Senate hearing Apr 21. NVDA hit fresh 52-week high Apr 27 (+4%); AMD ATH $352.99 Apr 24. Brent at $108 / WTI at $96 on **Iran's new US proposal to reopen Hormuz**.

items.headline: Totolink A8000RU CVE quad (7153/7154/7156/7202, all CVSS 9.8)
items.diff_narrative: **New: Totolink A8000RU CVE quad (7153/7154/7156/7202, all CVSS 9.8) — 4 critical OS-command-injection CVEs in 48h Apr 27-28 + Cisco ISE unauth-RCE advisory** — Previous report covered Apr 26 single Totolink CVE-2026-7037. Now **four additional Totolink A8000RU CVEs** drop in 48h (CVE-2026-7153 / 7154 / 7156 Apr 27, **CVE-2026-7202 Apr 28**), all 9.8, all unauthenticated remote command-injection. **Cisco ISE Unauthenticated Remote Code Execution Advisory** publishes in same window. SGLang CVE-2026-5760 **patch still not upstream** through Apr 28.

items.headline: Microsoft VibeVoice frontier-voice OSS surfaces Apr 27 + `talkie-1930-13b-base` ships Apr 28
items.diff_narrative: **New: Microsoft VibeVoice frontier-voice OSS surfaces Apr 27 + `talkie-1930-13b-base` ships Apr 28** — Previous report did not cover VibeVoice or Talkie. **VibeVoice** is Microsoft's Whisper-style speech-to-text family (MIT-licensed, speaker diarization in-model, 50+ languages, in Hugging Face Transformers since Mar 6). **Talkie-1930** (Apr 28) is a 13B language model trained on 260B tokens of pre-1931 historical text from Nick Levine, David Duvenaud, Alec Radford — first wide "vintage" LM release.

items.headline: Indirect-prompt-injection in the wild Apr 24-27
items.diff_narrative: **New: Indirect-prompt-injection in the wild Apr 24-27 — 10 fresh IPI payloads documented (Google + Forcepoint), OpenAI publicly concedes prompt-injection is here to stay** — Previous report covered Google's 32% YoY IPI rise. Now Forcepoint adds **10 fresh in-the-wild IPI payloads** including a fully-specified PayPal transaction targeting payment-capable agents and meta-tag namespace injection routing to a Stripe donation link; OpenAI publicly concedes the **prompt-injection class is permanent** as defenders lag.

items.headline: Figure AI scales to ~240 Figure 03 units/month + 90-min build cadence + $39B valuation; Tesla Optimus V3 reveal pushed to late July
items.diff_narrative: **New: Figure AI scales to ~240 Figure 03 units/month + 90-min build cadence + $39B valuation; Tesla Optimus V3 reveal pushed to late July** — Previous report covered Tesla Optimus V3 mid-year debut + Boston Dynamics Atlas. Now **Figure 03 monthly shipments doubling** (60→120→240 Feb-Mar-Apr), $39B valuation, BotQ scaling to 12,000+/year; **Tesla Optimus V3 reveal moved to late July / early August** ahead of Fremont production.

items.headline: Hugging Face Spaces ships HF Buckets persistent storage + agent-trace dataset uploads + ZeroGPU over-quota credits + volume mounting Apr 26-28
items.diff_narrative: **New: Hugging Face Spaces ships HF Buckets persistent storage + agent-trace dataset uploads + ZeroGPU over-quota credits + volume mounting Apr 26-28** — Previous report did not cover. Spaces now mount HF Buckets as persistent volumes; **agent traces from Claude Code / Codex / Pi upload directly to HF Datasets** with auto-detected trace format and dedicated viewer; ZeroGPU pre-paid credits ($1 / 10min) unlock past-quota usage for PRO users.

items.headline: Cerebras IPO mid-May window confirmed at $22B-$25B; OpenAI deal $20B+ / 750MW through 2028
items.diff_narrative: **New: Cerebras IPO mid-May window confirmed at $22B-$25B; OpenAI deal $20B+ / 750MW through 2028** — Previous report cited Cerebras filing Apr 17. Now **mid-May offering window** confirmed; underwriters Morgan Stanley / Citi / Barclays / UBS; 24% 2025 revenue from G42, 62% from MBZUAI (concentration risk).

items.headline: Multica v0.2.16 (Apr 24)
items.diff_narrative: **New: Multica v0.2.16 (Apr 24) — Chat V2 + right-click issue context menus + in-app feedback + redesigned Autopilot modal; v0.2.8 (Apr 20) added Kimi CLI runtime over ACP + per-agent model field** — Previous reports did not cover Multica through the entire Apr 23-27 window. Open-source vendor-neutral managed-agents orchestrator: assign issues to coding agents (Claude Code / Codex / OpenClaw / OpenCode / Hermes / Gemini / Pi / Cursor Agent) like to a colleague. Sits in the same orchestration layer as AgentCore Managed Harness and Claude Managed Agents.

items.headline: Unsloth v0.1.37-beta (Apr 23)
items.diff_narrative: **New: Unsloth v0.1.37-beta (Apr 23) — Studio UI/UX overhaul + Kimi-K2.6 + gpt-oss thinking toggle + Qwen3.6 Preserve Thinking + experimental API; v0.1.36-beta (Apr 8) Gemma-4-E2B 8 GB VRAM training, ~1.5x faster / ~60% less VRAM vs FA2** — Previous reports did not cover Unsloth through Apr 23-27 despite three releases (Apr 8 / 11 / 23) inside that window. Consumer-GPU fine-tuning + Studio's local-trainer rollout earn a standing slot under "Local LLM > Fine-tuning"; pairs with Bonsai-8B as the on-device-stack baseline.

items.headline: Hermes Agent v0.11.0 (Apr 23)
items.diff_narrative: **Continuing: Hermes Agent v0.11.0 (Apr 23) — extending traction Apr 26-28; Ink-TUI + Bedrock + GPT-5.5 + 5 new inference paths + 17th messaging platform** — Practitioner adoption deepening; >95,600 GitHub stars carries forward.

items.headline: OpenClaw v2026.4.25-beta train
items.diff_narrative: **Continuing: OpenClaw v2026.4.25-beta train — sub-24h cadence persists into Apr 28; voice-stack hardening + browser-automation safe-tab URLs + CDP-native role-snapshot** — No new minor jump beyond v2026.4.25 by Apr 28; the beta drumbeat is the story.

items.headline: SGLang CVE-2026-5760
items.diff_narrative: **Continuing: SGLang CVE-2026-5760 — unpatched into Apr 28; copy-paste class to vLLM/LMDeploy widening** — Maintainer coordination yielded no fix; the AI-Infra-KEV thesis tightens.

items.headline: Project Glasswing 12 launch partners + 40 critical-infrastructure orgs; Mythos Preview public-opinion debate vs Altman's "fear-based marketing" critique
items.diff_narrative: **Continuing: Project Glasswing 12 launch partners + 40 critical-infrastructure orgs; Mythos Preview public-opinion debate vs Altman's "fear-based marketing" critique** — Carry-forward; framing intact.
